intro: Alsophila × marcescens, also known as the Marcescent Tree Fern, is a hybrid tree fern species that belongs to the Cyathea and Alsophila genera. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Alsophila × marcescens include glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
